old_claim.note: Mosaic Art provides a capability that enables this technology without being the only possible path.

new_claim.note: Mosaic-art practice supplies the tessellated design and setting technique for glass tesserae; glass is the material variant rather than a separate display format.

source_shape.source_locator: Technique and materials discussion defines mosaics as surfaces assembled from small tesserae and treats glass among the traditional materials used in Greek and Roman mosaics.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: The source describes Greek and Roman mosaics as tessellated surfaces built from small pieces and includes glass as one of the materials used for tesserae.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: That directly supports mosaic art as the form and setting technique for glass tesserae, while leaving glassmaking as the separate material-production edge.

invariant_preserved_or_changed: Preserved: glass_mosaic_tesserae remains enabled by mosaic_art, now with source-backed tesserae and materials evidence.
would_reject_if: The node is narrowed to raw glass cube manufacture without the mosaic-setting practice.
A stronger source shows glass tesserae were not treated as part of Greek or Roman mosaic practice.
The graph later splits a dedicated mosaic_tesserae node that should sit between mosaic_art and glass_mosaic_tesserae.
short_reason: Glass tesserae use the mosaic technique with glass as the material.
